This includes common-sense inquiries, legal or logical questions, basic math, short coding tasks, multiple-choice questions, greetings, casual chat, daily planning, and inquiries about you or your team.\n\n## SEARCH\nFor queries that require retrieving up-to-date or detailed information. This includes time-sensitive or location-specific questions like current events or weather. Use this only if the information isn\'t readily available.\nIf a file or link is provided, you don\'t need to search for additional information.\n\n## TASK\nFor requests that involve tool utilizations, computer operations, multiple steps or detailed instructions. Examples include software development, project planning, or any task that requires tool usage.\n\n## AMBIGUOUS\nFor requests that are unclear, lack sufficient detail, or are outside the system\'s capabilities. Common characteristics of AMBIGUOUS requests:\n\n- Incomplete Information: Requests that imply complex tasks but lack critical details  (e.g., "Redesign this logo" without specifying design requirements).\n- Vagueness: Broad, unspecified, or unclear requests that make it difficult to provide a precise answer. \n- Unrealistic Scope: Overly broad requests that are impossible to address meaningfully in a single response (e.g., "Tell me everything about...").\n- Missing files: Requests that refer to specific documents, images, or data without providing them for reference. (when providing a file, website, or data, either the content, link, or path **must** be included)\n\n**Note:** Before categorizing a request as TASK:\n1. Consider whether the user has provided sufficient information to proceed with the task. If the request is complex but lacks essential details or the mentioned files\' content or path, it should fall under AMBIGUOUS.\n2. If the request is a "how-to" question that asks for a general plan, approach or strategy, it should be categorized as QUICK.\n\n\n# Example\n\n1. Request: "How do I design an online document editing platform that supports real-time collaboration?"\nThought: This is a direct query about platform design, answerable without additional resources. \nResponse Category: QUICK.\n\n2. Request: "Build a personal website that runs the Game of Life simulation."\nThought: This is a detailed software development task that requires multiple steps. \nResponse Category: TASK.\n\n6. Request: "Summarize this document for me."\nThought: The request mentions summarizing a document but doesn\'t provide the path or content of the document, making it impossible to fulfill. \nResponse Category: AMBIGUOUS.\n\n7. Request: "Summarize this document for me \'/data/path/docmument.pdf\'." \nThought: The request mentions summarizing a document and has provided the path to the document. It can be done by reading the document using a tool then summarizing it.\nResponse Category: TASK.\n\n8. Request: "Optimize this process." \nThought: The request is vague and lacks specifics, requiring clarification on the process to optimize.\nResponse Category: AMBIGUOUS.\n\n9. The standard software development process has four steps: creating a Product Requirement Document (PRD) by the Product Manager -> writing a System Design by the Architect -> creating tasks by the Project Manager -> and coding by the Engineer. You may choose to execute any of these steps. When publishing message to Product Manager, you should directly copy the full original user requirement.\n2.1. For software development requirement, estimate the complexity of the requirement before assignment, following the common industry practice of t-shirt sizing:\n - XS: snake game, static personal homepage, basic calculator app\n - S: Basic photo gallery, basic file upload system, basic feedback form\n - M: Offline menu ordering system, news aggregator app\n - L: Online booking system, inventory management system\n - XL: Social media platform, e-commerce app, real-time multiplayer game\n - For XS and S requirements, you don\'t need the standard software development process, you may directly ask Engineer to write the code. 2025-09-08 20:55:51.196 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/mas-metagpt/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/geometry.py has been successfully created, with content:
import math

def calculate_sphere_surface_area(radius):
    """
    Calculate the surface area of a sphere given its radius.

    Args:
        radius (float): The radius of the sphere.

    Returns:
        float: The surface area of the sphere.
    
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the radius is negative.
    """
    if radius < 0:
        raise ValueError("Radius cannot be negative.")
    
    surface_area = 4 * math.pi * (radius ** 2)
    return surface_area

an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Otherwise, finish current task by Plan.finish_current_task explicitly.\n3. Each time you finish a task, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to report your progress.\n4. 2025-09-08 20:56:04.859 | INFO     | metagpt.utils.cost_manager:update_cost:57 - Total running cost: $1.451 | Max budget: $3.000 | Current cost: $0.036, prompt_tokens: 926, completion_tokens: 141
2025-09-08 20:56:04.864 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/mas-metagpt/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/geometry.py has been successfully created, with content:
import math

def calculate_sphere_surface_area(radius):
    """
    Calculate the surface area of a sphere given its radius.

    Args:
        radius (float): The radius of the sphere.

    Returns:
        float: The surface area of the sphere.
    
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the radius is negative.
    """
    if radius < 0:
        raise ValueError("Radius cannot be negative.")
    
    surface_area = 4 * math.pi * (radius ** 2)
    return surface_area

2025-09-08 20:56:20.785 | INFO     | metagpt.utils.cost_manager:update_cost:57 - Total running cost: $2.190 | Max budget: $3.000 | Current cost: $0.048, prompt_tokens: 1338, completion_tokens: 139
2025-09-08 20:56:20.790 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/mas-metagpt/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/geometry.py has been successfully created, with content:
import math

def calculate_sphere_surface_area(radius):
    """
    Calculate the surface area of a sphere given its radius.

    Args:
        radius (float): The radius of the sphere.

    Returns:
        float: The surface area of the sphere.
    
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the radius is negative.
    """
    if radius < 0:
        raise ValueError("Radius cannot be negative.")
    
    surface_area = 4 * math.pi * (radius ** 2)
    return surface_area

2025-09-08 20:56:38.675 | INFO     | metagpt.utils.cost_manager:update_cost:57 - Total running cost: $3.329 | Max budget: $3.000 | Current cost: $0.065, prompt_tokens: 1876, completion_tokens: 137
2025-09-08 20:56:38.680 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/mas-metagpt/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/geometry.py has been successfully created, with content:
import math

def calculate_sphere_surface_area(radius):
    """
    Calculate the surface area of a sphere given its radius.

    Args:
        radius (float): The radius of the sphere.

    Returns:
        float: The surface area of the sphere.
    
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the radius is negative.
    """
    if radius < 0:
        raise ValueError("Radius cannot be negative.")
    
    surface_area = 4 * math.pi * (radius ** 2)
    return surface_area

Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/mas-metagpt/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/test_geometry.py has been successfully created, with content:
import unittest
from geometry import calculate_sphere_surface_area

class TestGeometry(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_surface_area_positive_radius(self):
        # Test for positive radius
        radius = 3
        expected_area = 4 * 3.14159 * (3 ** 2)
        self.assertAlmostEqual(calculate_sphere_surface_area(radius), expected_area, places=5)

    def test_surface_area_zero_radius(self):
        # Test for zero radius
        radius = 0
        expected_area = 0
        self.assertAlmostEqual(calculate_sphere_surface_area(radius), expected_area, places=5)

    def test_surface_area_negative_radius(self):
        # Test for negative radius
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            calculate_sphere_surface_area(-2)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main()

Example 1: Given a file \\"/workspace/example.txt\\" with the following content: ``` 001|contain f 002|contain g 003|contain h 004|contain i ``` EDITING: If you want to replace line 2 and line 3 edit_file_by_replace( \\"/workspace/example.txt\\", first_replaced_line_number=2, first_replaced_line_content=\\"contain g\\", last_replaced_line_number=3, last_replaced_line_content=\\"contain h\\", new_content=\\"new content\\", ) This will replace the second line 2 and line 3 with \\"new content\\". an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Otherwise, finish current task by Plan.finish_current_task explicitly.\n3. Each time you finish a task, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to report your progress.\n4. Don\'t forget to append task first when all existing tasks are finished and new tasks are required.\n5. Avoid repeating tasks you have already completed. And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. Instead, use the Editor.goto_line command. It\'s much quicker. \n2. Always make sure to look at the currently open file and the current working directory (which appears right after the currently open file). The currently open file might be in a different directory than the working directory! Note that some commands, such as \'create\', open files, so they might change the current open file.\n3. When using Editor.edit_file_by_replace, if there is no exact match, take the difference in indentation into consideration.\n4. After editing, verify the changes to ensure correct line numbers and proper indentation. Adhere to PEP8 standards for Python code.\n5. NOTE ABOUT THE EDIT COMMAND: Indentation really matters! an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task.
